sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: Ζεῦ Ζεῦ , τί λέγω , πόθεν ἄρξωμαι
sections.translation: O Zeus , O Zeus , what should I say ? Where shall I begin this prayer of mine , this appeal to the gods ? How in my loyal zeal can I succeed in finding words to match need ? Now is the moment when the blood-stained edges of the blades that lay men low

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: πειραὶ κοπάνων ἀνδροδαΐκτων
sections.translation: are utterly forever to destroy the house of Agamemnon . Or else , kindling a flaming light in the cause of freedom , Orestes will win both the rule over his realm and the rich possessions of his fathers .

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: πατέρων θ ἕξει [*] μέγαν ὄλβον .
sections.translation: Our gallant Orestes , with no one to assist him , is now to meet with two in such a contest . And may it be to triumph !

sections.label: Section 869
sections.original: ἐή , ὀτοτοτοῖ .
sections.translation: within Oh ! Oh ! O woe !

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: ἔα ἔα μάλα :
sections.translation: Ah ! Ah ! Alas ! What is happening ? What is being accomplished for our house ? Let us stand apart while the matter is still unsettled so that we may be considered blameless in these ills . For the issue of the fighting has now been decided . The Chorus withdraws to the side of the scene ; then a servant of Aegisthus rushes in

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: οἴμοι , πανοίμοι [*] δεσπότου πεπληγμένου : [*]
sections.translation: O woe , oh utter woe ! My master is slain ! O woe ! I cry yet again , for the third time . Aegisthus is no more ! Come , with all speed ! Unbar and open the women ’ s door ! And a strong arm indeed is needed , but not to help him who is already slain : what good is there in that ?

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: οὐχ ὥστ [*] ἀρῆξαι διαπεπραγμένῳ : [*] τί γάρ ;
sections.translation: Help ! Help ! Am I shouting to the deaf and fruitlessly wasting my voice on people who are asleep ? Where has Clytaemestra gone ? What is she doing ? Her own neck , near the razor ’ s edge , is now ready to fall beneath the stroke .

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: τί δ ἐστὶ χρῆμα ; τίνα βοὴν ἵστης δόμοις ;
sections.translation: What is this ? What cry for help are you raising in our house ?

sections.label: Section 886
sections.original: τὸν ζῶντα καίνειν τοὺς τεθνηκότας λέγω .
sections.translation: I tell you the dead are killing the living . [*]

sections.label: Section 887
sections.original: οἲ γώ . [*] ξυνῆκα τοὔπος ἐξ αἰνιγμάτων .
sections.translation: Ah ! Indeed I grasp the meaning of the riddle . We are to perish by treachery , just as we committed murder . Someone give me a battle-axe , and quickly ! Let us know if we are victors or vanquished :

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: εἰδῶμεν εἰ [*] νικῶμεν , ἢ νικώμεθα :
sections.translation: for I have even come to this in this wretched business . Exit Servant . The door is opened and the corpse of Aegisthus is discovered . Nearby stands Orestes , and at a distance Pylades

sections.label: Section 892
sections.original: σὲ καὶ ματεύω : τῷδε δ ἀρκούντως ἔχει .
sections.translation: It is you I seek . He over there has had enough .

sections.label: Section 893
sections.original: οἲ γώ . τέθνηκας , φίλτατ Αἰγίσθου βία .
sections.translation: Oh no ! My beloved , valiant Aegisthus ! You are dead !

sections.label: Section 894
sections.original: φιλεῖς τὸν ἄνδρα ; τοιγὰρ ἐν ταὐτῷ τάφῳ
sections.translation: You love this man ? Then you will lie in the same grave ,

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: κείσει . θανόντα δ οὔτι μὴ προδῷς ποτε .
sections.translation: and you will never abandon him in death .

sections.label: Section 896
sections.original: ἐπίσχες , ὦ παῖ , τόνδε δ αἴδεσαι , [*] τέκνον ,
sections.translation: Wait , my son ! Have pity , child , upon this breast at which many times while you slept you sucked with toothless gums the milk that nourished you .

sections.label: Section 899
sections.original: Πυλάδη , τί δράσω ; μητέρ αἰδεσθῶ κτανεῖν ;
sections.translation: Pylades , what shall I do ? Shall I spare my mother out of pity ?

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: ποῦ δὴ [*] τὰ λοιπὰ Λοξίου μαντεύματα
sections.translation: What then will become in the future of Loxias ’ oracles declared at Pytho , and of our sworn pact ? Count all men your enemies rather than the gods .

sections.label: Section 903
sections.original: κρίνω σὲ νικᾶν , καὶ παραινεῖς μοι καλῶς .
sections.translation: I judge you victor : you advise me well . To Clytaemestra Come , this way ! I mean to kill you by his very side . For while he lived , you thought him better than my father .

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: καὶ ζῶντα γάρ νιν κρείσσον [*] ἡγήσω πατρός :
sections.translation: Sleep with him in death , since you love him but hate the man you were bound to love .

sections.label: Section 908
sections.original: ἐγώ σ ἔθρεψα , σὺν [*] δὲ γηράναι θέλω .
sections.translation: It was I who nourished you , and with you I would grow old .

sections.label: Section 909
sections.original: πατροκτονοῦσα γὰρ ξυνοικήσεις ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: What ! Murder my father and then make your home with me ?

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: ἡ Μοῖρα τούτων , ὦ τέκνον , παραιτία .
sections.translation: Fate , my child , must share the blame for this .

sections.label: Section 911
sections.original: καὶ τόνδε τοίνυν Μοῖρ ἐπόρσυνεν [*] μόρον .
sections.translation: And fate now brings this destiny to pass .

sections.label: Section 912
sections.original: οὐδὲν σεβίζει [*] γενεθλίους ἀράς , τέκνον ;
sections.translation: Have you no regard for a parent ’ s curse , my son ?

sections.label: Section 913
sections.original: τεκοῦσα γάρ μ ἔρριψας ἐς τὸ δυστυχές .
sections.translation: You brought me to birth and yet you cast me out to misery .

sections.label: Section 914
sections.original: οὔτοι σ ἀπέρριψ ἐς δόμους δορυξένους .
sections.translation: No , surely I did not cast you out in sending you to the house of an ally .

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: αἰσχρῶς [*] ἐπράθην ὢν ἐλευθέρου πατρός .
sections.translation: I was sold in disgrace , though I was born of a free father .

sections.label: Section 916
sections.original: ποῦ δῆθ ὁ τῖμος , ὅντιν ἀντεδεξάμην ;
sections.translation: Then where is the price I got for you ?

sections.label: Section 917
sections.original: αἰσχύνομαί σοι [*] τοῦτ ὀνειδίσαι σαφῶς .
sections.translation: I am ashamed to reproach you with that outright .

sections.label: Section 918
sections.original: ἀλλ [*] εἴφ ὁμοίως καὶ πατρὸς τοῦ σοῦ μάτας .
sections.translation: But do not fail to proclaim the follies of that father of yours as well .

sections.label: Section 919
sections.original: μὴ λεγχε τὸν πονοῦντ ἔσω καθημένη .
sections.translation: Do not accuse him who suffered while you sat idle at home .

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: ἄλγος γυναιξὶν ἀνδρὸς εἴργεσθαι , τέκνον .
sections.translation: It is a grief for women to be deprived of a husband , my child .

sections.label: Section 921
sections.original: τρέφει δέ γ ἀνδρὸς μόχθος ἡμένας ἔσω .
sections.translation: Yes , but it is the husband ’ s toil that supports them while they sit at home .

sections.label: Section 922
sections.original: κτενεῖν ἔοικας , ὦ τέκνον , τὴν μητέρα .
sections.translation: You seem resolved , my child , to kill your mother .

sections.label: Section 923
sections.original: σύ τοι σεαυτήν , οὐκ ἐγώ , κατακτενεῖς .
sections.translation: You will kill yourself , not I .

sections.label: Section 924
sections.original: ὅρα , φύλαξαι μητρὸς ἐγκότους κύνας .
sections.translation: Take care : beware the hounds of wrath that avenge a mother .

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: τὰς τοῦ πατρὸς δὲ πῶς φύγω , παρεὶς τάδε ;
sections.translation: And how shall I escape my father ’ s if I leave this undone ?

sections.label: Section 926
sections.original: ἔοικα θρηνεῖν ζῶσα πρὸς τύμβον μάτην .
sections.translation: I see that though living I mourn in vain before a tomb . [*]

sections.label: Section 927
sections.original: πατρὸς γὰρ αἶσα τόνδε σοὐρίζει [*] μόρον . [*] [*]
sections.translation: Yes , for my father ’ s fate has marked out this destiny for you .

sections.label: Section 928
sections.original: οἲ γὼ τεκοῦσα τόνδ ὄφιν ἐθρεψάμην .
sections.translation: Oh no ! I myself bore and nourished this serpent !

sections.label: Section 929
sections.original: ἦ κάρτα μάντις οὑξ ὀνειράτων φόβος .
sections.translation: Yes , the terror from your dream was indeed a prophet . You killed him whom you should not ; so suffer what should not be .

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: ἔκανες [*] ὃν οὐ χρῆν , καὶ τὸ μὴ χρεὼν πάθε .
sections.translation: He forces Clytaemestra within ; Pylades follows

sections.label: Section 931
sections.original: στένω μὲν οὖν καὶ τῶνδε συμφορὰν διπλῆν .
sections.translation: Truly I grieve even for these in their twofold downfall . Yet since long-suffering Orestes has reached the peak of many deeds of blood , we would rather have it so , that the eye of the house should not be utterly lost .

sections.label: Section 1.935
sections.original: ἔμολε μὲν δίκα Πριαμίδαις χρόνῳ ,
sections.translation: As to Priam and his sons justice came at last in crushing retribution , so to Agamemnon ’ s house came a twofold lion , twofold slaughter . [*]

sections.label: Section 1.940
sections.original: ὁ πυθόχρηστος [*] φυγὰς
sections.translation: The exile , the suppliant of Pytho , has fulfilled his course to the utmost , justly urged on by counsels from the gods .

sections.label: Section 1.942
sections.original: ἐπολολύξατ ὦ δεσποσύνων δόμων
sections.translation: Oh raise a shout of triumph over the escape of our master ’ s house from its misery and the wasting of its wealth by two who were unclean ,

sections.label: Section 1.945
sections.original: δυσοίμου τύχας .
sections.translation: its grievous fortune !

sections.label: Section 1.946
sections.original: ἔμολε δ ᾧ μέλει κρυπταδίου μάχας
sections.translation: And he has come whose part is the crafty vengeance of stealthy attack , and in the battle his hand was guided by her who is in very truth daughter of Zeus , breathing murderous wrath on her foes .

sections.label: Section 1.950
sections.original: προσαγορεύομεν βροτοὶ τυχόντες καλῶσ —
sections.translation: We mortals aim true to the mark when we call her Justice . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.953
sections.original: τάνπερ ὁ Λοξίας ὁ Παρνασίας [*]
sections.translation: The commands proclaimed loudly by Loxias , tenant of the mighty cavern shrine of Parnassus , assail with guileless guile

sections.label: Section 2.960
sections.original: ἄξια δ [*] οὐρανοῦχον ἀρχὰν σέβειν :
sections.translation: It is right to revere the rule of heaven .

sections.label: Section 2.961
sections.original: πάρα τὸ [*] φῶς ἰδεῖν .
sections.translation: Look , the light has come , and I am freed from the cruel curb that restrained our household . House , rise up ! You have lain too long prostrate on the ground .

sections.label: Section 2.965
sections.original: τάχα δὲ παντελὴς χρόνος ἀμείψεται
sections.translation: But soon time that accomplishes all will pass the portals of our house , and then all pollution will be expelled from the hearth by cleansing rites that drive out calamity . The dice of fortune will turn as they fall and lie

sections.label: Section 2.970
sections.original: ἰδεῖν [ ἀκοῦσαι [*] ] πρευμενεῖς
sections.translation: with faces all lovely to behold , favorably disposed to whoever stays in our house .

sections.label: Section 2.972
sections.original: πάρα τὸ φῶς ἰδεῖν . [*]
sections.translation: Look , the light has come ,

sections.label: Section 973
sections.original: ἴδεσθε χώρας τὴν διπλῆν τυραννίδα
sections.translation: Behold this pair , oppressors of the land , who murdered my father and ransacked my house ! They were majestic then , when they sat on their thrones ,

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: σεμνοὶ μὲν ἦσαν ἐν θρόνοις τόθ ἥμενοι ,
sections.translation:
and are lovers even now , as one may judge by what has happened to them , and their oath holds true to their pledges . Together they vowed a league of death against my unhappy father , and together they vowed to die , and they have kept their promise well .
But now regard again , you who hear this account of ills ,

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: ἴδεσθε δ αὖτε , τῶνδ ἐπήκοοι κακῶν ,
sections.translation: the device for binding my unhappy father , with which his hands were manacled , his feet fettered . Spread it out ! Stand around in a circle , and display this covering for a man , that the Father may see — not mine , but he who surveys all this , the Sun —

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: δροίτης κατασκήνωμα ; δίκτυον μὲν οὖν ,
sections.translation: that he may see the impious work of my own mother , that he may be my witness in court that I justly pursued this death , my own mother ’ s . For I do not speak of Aegisthus ’ death : he has suffered the penalty prescribed for adulterers .
